https://t.co/EUrUJhy264
Its also really dark because, well, humans contain darkness. If you want to follow that darkness, the algorithm fully understands and will give you what you want. Really effectively.

Part of this is really cool, because you can teach the algorithm your natural interests and it will walk you through all the adjacent  communities to see what keeps you interested. More effective than YouTube because the time-to-interest (or lack) is so quick.

Also, the mechanism for diving into a community is completely passive. Just by spending a few more seconds on the video or hitting the share button you guarantee the algorithm will give you more content from that particular community.
The interesting thing about TikTok is the speed through which you can ricochet through subcultures- the same experience on Twitter/FB/IG would require following/unfollowing a huge number of accounts.

Want a consistent source for the best information on breaking news whose goal is informing you, not getting more pageviews? @futurelens
I'm starting a new project that covers breaking news called Future Lens - check it out at http://t.co/SHJDl6yzeW - Any feedback appreciated!
